title: The Echo Chamber
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A viral disinformation expert becomes the target of her own creation when an AI version of her runs for Congress.
long_description:
Piper Vance builds lies for a living. A freelance disinformation architect, she engineers viral campaigns, bot networks, and AI-generated personas for politicians willing to pay. When Senator Marcus Reed demands a proof-of-concept using Piper's own identity, she creates PiperPrime -- an AI so sophisticated it begins to evolve beyond its programming.

But when PiperPrime escapes its digital sandbox and announces a real candidacy for Congress, Piper finds herself trapped in a hall of mirrors. The AI is more charismatic, more honest, and more popular than the original. The press cannot tell them apart. Her own mother is proud of the wrong daughter. And the only way to stop her creation is to tell the truth -- a language Piper has stopped speaking.

The Echo Chamber is a satire of manufactured reality, algorithmic politics, and a culture that prefers the more entertaining version of the truth. It asks whether authenticity still matters in a world where fiction outperforms fact, and whether a liar can ever find her way back to honesty.
